title: tmuxcc server-proxy → client messages
description: All control-plane messages pushed by the server-proxy to a connected client. The server-proxy is a per-tmux-socket discovery and lifecycle service. Status: Stage 0 placeholder — the server-proxy process, transport, and runtime are implemented in Stage 2 (tc-j9c.3). Types are defined now so the file layout matches the target shape.

ServerProxyCapabilitiesMessage.description: Server-proxy capabilities advertisement, sent once at handshake time. direction: server-proxy→client.

ServerProxySessionInfo.description: A session as known to the server-proxy. Counts are static at snapshot/delta time. tc-295a.4 (W1.3): enriched with tmuxccMarked, paneCount, lastActivity.

windowCount.description: Number of windows currently in this session at snapshot/delta time.

attachedClientCount.description: Number of tmuxcc clients currently attached at snapshot/delta time.

tmuxccMarked.description: True iff the session carries the @tmuxcc 1 user option (created or claimed by tmuxcc); false for foreign sessions.

paneCount.description: Total panes across all windows in this session at snapshot/delta time (tmux list-panes -a).

lastActivity.description: Unix epoch (seconds) of the most-recent activity in this session (#{session_activity}); 0 when unknown.

ServerProxySnapshotMessage.description: Full session-list snapshot, sent once by the server-proxy immediately after the capabilities handshake. direction: server-proxy→client.

sessions.description: All sessions known to this server-proxy at snapshot time.

ServerProxySessionAddedMessage.description: A new session has become visible to the server-proxy. Only pushed when the "sessions-watch" feature is negotiated. direction: server-proxy→client. tc-295a.4 (W1.3): carries the enriched session fields.

ServerProxySessionRemovedMessage.description: A session has disappeared from the server-proxy. The server-proxy reaps the session-proxy (if any) before emitting this. direction: server-proxy→client.

ServerProxySessionRenamedMessage.description: A session was renamed. Emitted alongside the session-proxy wire's session.renamed. direction: server-proxy→client.

ServerProxyExitingMessage.description: The server-proxy is about to perform a DESIGNED self-exit (tc-xnay / tc-ymxe). Broadcast immediately before shutdown() runs. Best-effort: may be dropped if transport is already closed. Distinguishes designed quiescence from unexpected death for both self-spawned and externally-started brokers. direction: server-proxy→client (broadcast).

reason.description: Why the broker is exiting. Known values: "idle" (zero clients + zero session-proxy children for hysteresis window), "tmux-gone" (tmux server vanished). Open-ended for forward compatibility — the presence of this message is the load-bearing signal, not the reason string.

ServerProxyCommandOkPayload.description: Successful server-proxy command result payload. Per-kind: session.claim/session.create → {sessionId, endpoint, created}; session.destroy → {}; pane.attach → {sessionId, endpoint, paneId}; server-proxy.info → {info}.

endpoint.description: Opaque connection string (unix socket path). Clients pass to createSessionProxyTransport(endpoint).

paneId.description: Echoed target paneId for a pane.attach response. The server-proxy does not validate pane existence.

created.description: True if this claim/create minted the tmux session. False if attached to pre-existing. Absent on session.destroy / pane.attach responses.

info.description: Diagnostics snapshot for a server-proxy.info response (tc-k6v). Absent on all other command kinds.

ServerProxyCommandResponseMessage.description: The server-proxy's response to a ServerProxyCommandRequestMessage. direction: server-proxy→client.

code.description: Error code. Known values: protocol.unknown-message, protocol.malformed, protocol.version-mismatch, session.not-found, session.name-taken, tmux.unavailable, internal.

ServerProxyMessage.description: Discriminated union of all messages the server-proxy pushes to the client. Narrow on `type`. Note: ErrorMessage (type: "error") is shared with the session-proxy wire — use the session-proxy/server-push schema's ErrorMessage definition.
